content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach consistently names how the client is showing up, not just what they're saying, and the reads precede the questions. Turn 4 catches the rush from feeling-word to strategy ('you moved pretty quickly from stressed to let's make a list'). Turn 6 names the contradiction between the said and the shown ('stressed is just the baseline — and then you listed about six things that are actually wrong'). Turn 8 catches the self-answer-and-deflect ('you just answered your own question and then changed the subject'). Turn 10 distinguishes asserted belief from felt belief ('you don't quite sound like you believe it yourself'). Turn 12 names the pattern of admitting and immediately retracting. Turn 14 catches the reach-for-the-next-thing in real time ('you almost got there'). These are specific AoA reads — the rush signature, the linguistic caveat, defensiveness-as-identity-signal — named at the moment they're live, and each read grounds the next question rather than the coach asking blind. The only thing missing is an explicit somatic read (breath, body), but the linguistic and pacing reads are precise and timely.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens with a feeling question but no read named yet."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the rush signature explicitly — the pivot from feeling to list — and grounds the next question in it."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Distinguishes the said from the shown: 'baseline' vs. the six wrong things just listed."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the self-answer-then-deflect move in real time."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the gap between assertion and conviction ('you don't quite sound like you believe it yourself')."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the pattern: describes it exactly, then explains why it doesn't count. Pattern-level read of the defense."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the reach to pick up pace again ('you almost got there') — reads the avoidance live."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Reads are linguistic/energetic rather than somatic, but they are specific, timely, and consistently precede the inquiry. The 'you moved pretty quickly' and 'you almost got there' moments are textbook rush-signature reads."
}
